title: artemisia campestris subsp. campestris
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. campestris, also known as field wormwood or field sagewort, is a subspecies of the plant Artemisia campestris, which is a flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which contain compounds like camphor and thujone. The health benefits of Artemisia campestris subsp. campestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
